entry.title: Washington Post: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: G-7 to back Ukraine ‘as long as it takes’; Zelensky seeks anti-aircraft systems
  • G-7 leaders seek price cap on Russian oil to ‘starve’ Putin’s war effort
  • Russia defaults on foreign debt for first time since 1918: What to know
  • Photos found near bombed-out Kyiv apartment tell a family’s story
  • The Supreme Court turns the U.S. into a cautionary tale
  • Bullfight stands collapse, causing deaths and scores of injuries
  • Russian missiles hit Kyiv, shattering the capital’s relative calm
  • Strikes hit Kyiv; Biden says G-7 will ban Russian gold imports
  • Biden: G-7 to ban Russian gold imports to deny Putin revenue for Ukraine war
  • A bloody retreat as Ukrainian unit hit by Russian cluster bombs
  • Johnson, Trudeau joke about going shirtless to threaten Putin with ‘our pecs’
  • The wild history of the Bavarian castle hosting this week’s G-7 summit
  • Live updates | 5 top emerging economies join G-7 summit
  • UN oceans conference eyes steps toward high seas agreement
  • Czech bullet train collides with engine, 1 dead, 5 injured
  • Pakistani army says shootout with militants killed 2 troops
  • Norway: Suspect in deadly Pride shooting agrees to custody
  • Sudan: Ethiopia kills 7 captive Sudanese soldiers, civilian
  • Knife attacker kills 1, wounds 5 at German asylum shelter
  • Detained LGBTQ activists in Istanbul Pride being released
  • Death toll of children in Afghanistan quake rises to 155
  • People's war: Ukraine, Russia crowd-funding gear for troops
  • Where Texas teachers carry guns, a community feels more secure
  • A Minnesota publisher just gave away his newspaper to fight in Ukraine
  • Shooting fears sow chaos at San Francisco, New York City Pride events
  • Abortion protests continue after Supreme Court ends Roe v. Wade; Biden criticizes court’s ‘terrible decisions’
  • Your questions about the end of Roe, answered
  • GOP lawmaker calls Roe ruling ‘victory for white life’ as Trump rally cheers
  • A women’s clinic run by two generations of women braces for the post-Roe era
  • Biden says America is leading. On abortion, Europeans disagree.
  • Fed interest rate hikes just made national debt a lot more expensive
  • Martin ‘Art’ Walsh, influential land-use lawyer, dies at 78
  • With clock ticking, battle over tech regulation intensifies
  • Seeking an abortion? Here’s how to avoid leaving a digital trail.
  • Each state has its own rules governing estate taxes
  • Three-bedroom townhouse-style condo in Lorton lists below $395,000
  • Crypto’s High Ideals About Freedom Take a Beating
  • The UK Risks Entering a New Era of Economic Decline
  • Should European Banking Really Be More Like US Banking?
  • Many Winters Are Coming. Start Saving Energy Now.
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • A ‘Price Cap’ on Russian Oil — What Would That Mean?
  • Understanding Crypto Bridges and $1 Billion in Thefts
  • Why Margin Calls and Bot Liquidations Are Roiling Crypto
  • Ron DeSantis’s snub of Trump is a 2024 challenge
  • A question for those who say more faith will prevent gun violence: How?
  • The Supreme Court declares war on modern America
  • What the Nixon pardon tells us about the perils of letting Trump walk
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 27 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-27 11:07AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Power company to remove remains of bridge and finish laying cables a week after fire that left 160,000 Hong Kong households without electricity
  • Number of coronavirus cases linked to Hong Kong nursing homes rises as health experts call for unvaccinated elderly residents to get jabs
  • HK Express flight lands at Hong Kong airport with smoke billowing from plane, no injuries reported
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping expected to begin 2-day trip to Hong Kong on Thursday ‘but will not stay in the city overnight’
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 teens allegedly involved in clash that led to cleaner’s death cannot be held liable for murder, judge rules
  • International schoolteachers on the future of education, from pandemic-era online lessons with Zoom and Google tools, to AR and VR – and the importance of social skills and empathy too
  • Hong Kong-mainland China ties after 25 years: a chance for a reset or will underlying unease fester?
  • Rupture and reset (2017-2022): Hong Kong’s turbulent years and Covid-19 battle in 25 photos
  • Hong Kong’s Cathay Pacific back on hiring spree in anticipation of take-off in global air travel, but faces recruitment challenges
  • Why this 25-year-old is not leaving Hong Kong
  • Moderna seeking Hong Kong regulatory approval for Omicron-targeted Covid-19 booster vaccine
  • Hong Kong police’s elite squads to get new uniforms for better protection, easier identification during operations
  • Two years on, national security law proves its worth in safer, more stable Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong’s largest journalist group insists it operates in accordance with law after being asked by authorities to justify activities
  • No insurance payout over capsizing of Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant, owner says
  • Hong Kong PLA garrison to be combat-ready for ‘toughest and most complicated’ situations amid security threats, commander vows
  • Rent control law fails to help Hong Kong’s poorest and leaves loopholes for landlords, advocacy group says
  • Hong Kong’s finance chief says he has ‘stood firm’ in defending national, financial security while assessing tenure
  • National, Hong Kong flags found damaged at public housing estates, some with black marks on yellow stars of banners
  • Tuvalu minister pulls out of UN Ocean Conference after China blocks its Taiwanese delegates
  • ByteDance’s metaverse investments continue with acquisition of Chinese virtual social platform PoliQ
  • Hong Kong star Jackie Chan attracts 130 million on Kuaishou as Chinese platforms deploy celebrity live-streams to lure users
  • Chinese prototype a precursor to hypersonic passenger travel anywhere in the world in 2 hours, says team
  • Hong Kong adjusts to new economic role after blazing trail for others
  • China changes law so it can act if country is insulted at sporting events
  • Human cost of China’s zero-Covid policy measured in stress, anxiety
  • China questions the safety of open source code amid sanctions and tech dependency risks, but can it build a viable alternative?
  • Higher oil prices help Angola pay off debts to Chinese banks
  • Biden unveils US$600 billion global infrastructure plan at G7 summit to counter China’s Belt and Road
  • Heat turned up on China-Japan relationship with maritime disputes over islands and alliances
  • Coronavirus: China’s economic slowdown looks like a ‘lifetime of debt’ for some citizens
  • Under Xi Jinping China-Russia ties are defined by global interests and unity in opposition, report finds
  • Asean special envoy appeals to Myanmar junta to spare Suu Kyi prison
  • Singapore’s return to tighter Covid rules cannot be ruled out, deputy PM Wong says
  • South Korean Navy Seal broke the law to fight in Ukraine, calls it ‘the Wild West’
  • Japan’s snub of talks with South Korea on sidelines of Nato summit a ‘missed opportunity’
  • Is the US setting up an ‘Asia-style Nato’? North Korea thinks so
  • Japan’s Kishida makes veiled China reference as he warns G7 of ‘attempts to change the status quo by force’
  • Who is Louise Araneta-Marcos, the Philippines’ new first lady?
  • Australia hosts ‘historic’ Nasa launch – first from commercial site outside US
  • China’s education crackdown spurs Japan moves for international schools
  • Indonesia ‘democracy, human rights at risk’ as row over criminal code heats up
  • Ukraine: ‘Help end the war by year’s end’, Zelensky urges world leaders
  • Nato to pledge aid to Baltics and Ukraine, urge Türkiye to let in Sweden and Finland
  • Roe vs Wade: America’s rival camps dig in for fight after abortion ruling
  • Ukraine war: Russia slips into default zone as payment deadline expires
  • UN drugs report: Cannabis use has risen with legalisation and Covid-19 lockdowns
  • Dozens arrested in Istanbul as LGBT marchers try to defy Pride ban
  • Suspect in Norway Pride Month shooting ‘refuses to talk to police’
  • Motown singer Diana Ross spreads the love at UK’s Glastonbury music festival
  • New York ‘duck sauce killer’ Glenn Hirsch could be freed on bail
  • Iran launches rocket into space as nuclear talks to resume
  • South Africa police investigate deaths of 21 teens at pub
  • Prince Charles’ US$3 million bags-of-cash claim, denies any wrongdoing
  • Pride parades march on with new urgency amid calls for changes in law
  • Designer perfumes hit by war and weather as vital ingredient becomes ‘liquid gold’
  • Hong Kong can be a hub, not just for capital going into China, but also funds coming out of mainland for M&A, ex-SFC chairman says
  • How China can protect its economy – and the world’s – amid US recession headwinds
  • Alibaba, Haidilao lead Hong Kong stocks to near three-month high on China recovery signals as Beijing eases lockdown
  • Hongkongers join bespoke Japan property tours to scout for investment opportunities amid yen’s slump
  • Taiwan stocks facing ‘big bully’ prompt Goldman retreat as cross-strait tensions inflict 20 to 40 per cent fund losses
  • US, Europe travel chaos a preview of the pain and indignities to come for Asia
  • Why central bank digital currencies would mean the end of data privacy
  • How parents can help their children get ready for a digital future
  • The 10 best K-dramas of 2022 … so far, from Our Blues and Link: Eat, Love, Kill to Money Heist: Korea – Joint Economic Area
  • Who are Lionesses? Queer K-pop boy band who want to be the LGBTQ+ icons they wish they’d had growing up
  • Pandemic anger, ‘panger’, is a real mental health concern. Expert suggests 4 steps to deal with your emotions
  • Thailand’s temples the star of luxury boat trip from Bangkok to the ancient kingdom of Ayutthaya
  • Actress Fala Chen on Irma Vep, Shang-Chi, parallels with Maggie Cheung and the Louis Vuitton catsuit she had to wear on set
  • School in China ditches sleeping on desks at nap time for health reasons and lets students set up their own tents to sleep in
  • Chicken, the ‘fowl of five virtues’ in Chinese lore, and why Singapore got into a flap over a shortage of the birds
  • How an old empty Hong Kong building became a homeless shelter and could serve as the template to get more people off the streets
  • From Zendaya to Chloe Grace Moretz, women in suits and ties are everywhere – as men change out of theirs
  • Organic cotton types, facts, certification and why you should buy clothes made from it
  • Virgil Abloh’s legacy at Louis Vuitton’s Paris Fashion Week menswear show: the bold spring/summer 2023 collection is the fashion house’s first not created by the late designer
  • 8 best modern Chinese restaurants in Hong Kong: from favourites Man Ho and Mott 32, to celebrity chef Vicky Cheng’s Wing and fine dining Cantonese at Rùn
  • 5 K-pop reunions that prove 2nd generation groups are forever: Kara surprised fans with comeback rumours, 2PM released a mini-album, and 2NE1’s Coachella gig
  • 6 facts about Seo In-guk, star of Netflix’s Café Minamdang – the ‘chameleon’ K-drama actor loves food, music, his mum … and older women
  • 6 best cities for executive digital nomads: Miami, Lisbon, Dubai and Barcelona top Savills’ first ranking of locations ideal for long-term remote working
  • Inside South Indian star Nayanthara’s US$22 million net worth – the Netrikann actress lavishes husband Vignesh Shivan with gifts, boasts a huge real estate empire and gets around by private jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-27 11:03AM.
entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v South Africa: women’s Test match, day one – live!
  • Wimbledon 2022: rain delay with Djokovic, Raducanu and Murray to play – live!
  • Letting Russia win in Ukraine would be ‘absolutely chilling’, says Boris Johnson
  • England chasing 296 to win third Test against New Zealand: rain delay on day five – live!
  • Israel and Saudi Arabia ‘in talks over joint defence against Iran’
  • Boris Johnson defends Northern Ireland protocol bill ahead of Commons vote
  • Russia-Ukraine war: civilians told to evacuate Lysychansk; Kremlin says ‘not our problem’ if bond payments blocked by sanctions – live
  • Top Gun: Maverick takes in over $1bn, the highest box office of 2022 so far
  • Crossword roundup: Tory party pickles and how would you clue chicken kyiv?
  • We Americans are dancing on the Titanic. Our iceberg is not far away | Francine Prose
  • Black women could see a 33% increase in pregnancy-related deaths post-Roe. Why? | Tayo Bero
  • ‘I’m very afraid’: same-sex couples express anxiety about a post-Roe landscape
  • How we met: ‘I had a feeling that if we started dating, we’d end up married’
  • Rachel Roddy’s recipe for gnocchi alla Sorrentina | A kitchen in Rome

  • Alec Baldwin to interview Woody Allen on Instagram Live
  • Zelenskiy meets G7 leaders as US plans to give air defence system to Ukraine
  • Glass act: critics see right through Adelaide rental with bathroom ‘cube’ built next to kitchen

  • ‘People are walking off the job’: workers blame American Red Cross for US blood shortage
  • A pilgrim’s (rather slow) progress on Sicily’s most recent long-distance trail
  • Petr Cech to step down as Chelsea’s technical and performance advisor
  • Alarm in Beijing after announcement zero-Covid policy may last five years
  • Japanese told to turn off lights to save energy amid Tokyo heatwave
  • ‘Watergate for streaming era’: how the January 6 panel created gripping hearings
  • Gareth Bale may need MLS and LAFC more than they need him
  • Lauren Boebert: could the rightwing extremist be re-elected to Congress?
  • Ukrainian spouses in Gaza suffer from double conflict
  • Let’s call the overturning of Roe v Wade what it is: state-sanctioned forced birth | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
  • Iga Swiatek thrives by taking care of mental as well as physical strength | Sean Ingle
  • ‘This was properly amazing work’: the artist’s life’s work found in a skip
  • If it cared, TikTok could stop itself being used to stir up tribal hatred in Kenya | Odanga Madung
  • Republicans have hijacked the US supreme court. It’s time to expand it | David Daley
  • ‘You get goosebumps from the data’: hopes rise for new malaria vaccine
  • Can you solve it? Are you smart enough to work for Elon Musk?
  • ‘All her movies are nonfiction’: remembering the life and work of Nora Ephron
  • The Sheffield council house that became a pioneer of rock recording
  • ‘Karaoke? I’m absolutely dreadful’: Katherine Jenkins’ honest playlist
  • Viruses survive in fresh water by ‘hitchhiking’ on plastic, study finds
  • Windfall tax on Covid profits could ease ‘catastrophic’ food crisis, says Oxfam
  • ‘It’s everyone coming back together’: why 200,000 of us couldn’t wait to get back to Glastonbury
  • New Zealand ‘grossly underprepared’ as deadly drug fentanyl causes string of overdoses
  • Collapse of bullfight stands in Colombia leaves four dead, hundreds injured
  • ‘There is a lot of excitement’: Tour de France comes to Denmark
  • Avalanche win first Stanley Cup since 2001 with Game 6 win over Lightning
  • Shipwreck of US destroyer ‘Sammy B’ becomes deepest ever discovered
  • ‘Incredible milestone’: Nasa launches rocket from Australian space centre
  • Garbage island no more: how one Japanese community triumphed over a toxic waste dump
  • Women’s Euro 2022 team guide No 1: Austria
  • Many US companies move to pay travel costs for employees seeking abortions
  • Gold miner in Canada finds mummified 35,000-year-old woolly mammoth
  • What’s at stake in the extradition of Julian Assange?
  • ‘Older, whiter and smaller’: new census data to show how Covid changed Australia
  • Murdered British journalist Dom Phillips laid to rest in Brazil
  • Prince Charles: calls for investigations into ‘cash in bags’ controversy
  • Oslo shootings won’t stop fight against hate, says Norway’s prime minister
  • Calls for investigation over deaths in Moroccan-Spanish border crossing
  • G7 grapples with packed agenda of world turned upside down
  • How I cope with feelings of envy by saying the Arabic word ‘mashallah’
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.content:

entry.title: NY Times: 27 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
